script:
Morning. Here is where things stand.

The market. Databricks has stepped into our world. Their new CustomerLake builds identity resolution right into the lakehouse, and HP and Circle K are already trying it. Around the same time, Snowflake laid out its own native identity matching and put six billion dollars into AWS for the compute agents will run on. Read together, the big platforms are moving toward identity from above, while the money follows the agents. Hightouch also shipped campaign tooling you can drive from Claude, so the composable crowd is selling finished work now, not just plumbing.

The house. Our belief that identity is the substrate is holding at 72, though this was a tougher week for it. CustomerLake pushes against it, and it is well sourced. The good news alongside that: Snowflake's own report named us the identity leader in its ecosystem. One thing to keep watching is whether identity slowly commoditizes. That is the question the next couple of quarters will really turn on.

The week. Four of the five moves are in good shape, and Derek's coexistence note is agreed and moving. Retention is the one that needs some care: its move is still just proposed and has slipped two weeks now. Amy has the unblock, and the holdup is clear enough, the usage data for six accounts is still in the old warehouse.

The call. The one worth deciding today is how we respond to CustomerLake in public. A quiet, confident coexistence line this week would get ahead of it. Feels like Kabir's call, and tomorrow would be a good moment for it.
